component.name: SynchroSonic
component.summary: Linux-first LAN audio casting and receiver control

ul.li: Aligned release metadata and packaging documentation for SynchroSonic v0.1.11.
Rechecked Linux release artifact expectations for AppImage, Debian, Flatpak, and tarball outputs.
Corrected stale version references from earlier release preparation passes.

description.p: Linux packaging polish and metadata alignment update for the SynchroSonic desktop app.

This release keeps the packaging, documentation, and release-facing metadata aligned on version 0.1.10, expands the documented Linux package targets, and describes preview-only runtime paths more honestly.

SynchroSonic is a Linux-first GTK4/libadwaita desktop application for capturing system audio, discovering receivers, casting over the local network, running a receiver mode, and inspecting stream, sync, and buffer health.

The current release track focuses on Linux PipeWire tooling, LAN transport, and explicit diagnostics. Bluetooth support is intentionally limited to selecting local Linux playback outputs exposed by PipeWire.
The current public release line uses version 0.1.11 and the canonical AudioShare GitHub repository for source, releases, and issue tracking.
